data.title: Volato Announces Receipt of Continued Listing Standards Notice From NYSE American
data.description: Atlanta-based Volato Group, Inc. (NYSE American: SOAR) disclosed today receiving a notice from NYSE American LLC on March 17, 2026, indicating non-compliance with listing standards. The notice, citing Section 1003(a)(i) of the NYSE American Company Guide, specifies a requirement for stockholders’ equity to be a minimum of $2.0 million. As per the notice, Volato has fallen short of this benchmark.

data.title: Verde Clean Fuels, Inc. Announces New CEO and Engagement of Financial Advisor to Evaluate Strategic Alternatives
data.description: Houston-based company Verde Clean Fuels, Inc. (NASDAQ: VGAS) has appointed George Burdette as its new Chief Executive Officer (CEO). The company has also engaged Roth Capital Partners as its financial advisor to help evaluate strategic alternatives. These moves are part of Verde's ongoing efforts to restructure and reduce costs. Burdette takes over from Ernie Miller, who is stepping down from the position.

data.title: The Vita Coco Company Set to Join S&P SmallCap 600
data.description: On March 20, 2026, the Vita Coco Company Inc. (NASD: COCO) announced it will join the S&P SmallCap 600 index, taking the place of TEGNA Inc. (NYSE: TGNA), starting on March 25. As part of the changes, Nexstar Media Group Inc. (NASD: NXST), a current member of the S&P MidCap 400, has acquired...

data.title: BYUtv Wins 2 Children's and Family Emmy® Awards from 14 Nominations
data.description: BYUtv in Provo, Utah announced on March 20, 2026, the nomination of several titles for awards. The nominated titles, which include "The Primrose Railway Children," "Secrets at Red Rocks," "Malory Towers," and "All-Round Champion," showcase the network's commitment to providing inspiring family entertainment. BYUtv, known for its free streaming and availability on cable and satellite platforms, has garnered two nominations as a recognition of their dedication to producing quality content.
